title: Former Sears store on Syracuse’s South Side to be turned into semiconductor workforce training center
summary: New York State is investing $8.5 million to transform the vacant 1929 art deco Sears building at 1300 S. Salina Street into the ON-RAMP Workforce Innovation Hub, a semiconductor and advanced manufacturing training center anchored by Micron Technology's $100 billion regional commitment. The hub, part of a $200 million statewide initiative, will address workforce development barriers and support Syracuse's South Side economic revitalization alongside nearby manufacturing and community health investments.
department_refs: Office of the Chief Innovation Officer

political_signal.description: State-level championship of upstate revitalization and advanced manufacturing workforce development; framed as addressing generational disinvestment in South Side neighborhoods; aligns with broader I-90 corridor advanced manufacturing strategy anchored by major corporate commitment.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts validated: $8.5M (hub funding), $100B (Micron commitment), $22.5M (health center), $200M (broader ON-RAMP initiative)
Historic preservation angle significant: 1929 art deco landmark reuse after 51-year vacancy
Geographic cluster: South Salina Street corridor showing signs of revitalization (JMA 2022, health center, mixed-use Salina 1st project)
Implementation note: Staff temporarily housed at CenterState CEO downtown while site redeveloped
Recommend cross-reference with: Micron incentive package, I-90 corridor planning, CenterState CEO partnership agreements, local zoning/permitting for adaptive reuse
